message:
fcp-host/batch: fail-closed on unknown-tool operations in BatchZoneValidator

Previously `BatchZoneValidator::effective_zone` returned
`operation.zone.or_else(registry.get_zone(&operation.tool)).unwrap_or(&self.agent_zone)`.
If an operation carried no explicit zone AND its tool name was not in
the `ZoneRegistry`, the validator silently defaulted to the current
agent zone — so the operation would be considered zone-accessible by
definition, regardless of which zone the agent was in. That is a
bypass: an operation naming a tool the registry doesn't recognise
could smuggle work through zone checks that a registered tool would
have been blocked from performing.

Fix: make `effective_zone` fail-closed by returning
`Option<&ZoneId>` and surfacing the `None` case as an explicit
"missing zone mapping" preflight error.

Changes in crates/fcp-host/src/batch.rs:

* `effective_zone` now returns `Option<&ZoneId>`: `operation.zone`
  first, then the registry lookup; no `unwrap_or(&self.agent_zone)`.
* `validate` walks ops once, partitioning into `violations`
  (zone-accessible check failed) and `unknown_tools`
  (`effective_zone` returned None). The error precedence is:
  - if any operations are unknown_tools → `PreflightFailed("missing
    zone mapping for operations: <ids>")`;
  - else if any are violations → `PreflightFailed("zone boundary
    violations for operations: <ids>")`;
  - else Ok.
  Unknown-tool failures take precedence because they are a registry
  hygiene bug and we want the operator to see them explicitly before
  triaging any zone violations.
* `group_by_zone` now emits `"<unresolved>"` for operations whose
  tool cannot be mapped to a zone, so the grouping stays total.
  Downstream reporting can render or filter that bucket.
* Test rename + flip: `zone_validator_unknown_tool_passes` →
  `zone_validator_unknown_tool_rejects_missing_mapping`. It now
  constructs an empty `ZoneRegistry`, submits an op with an unknown
  tool, and asserts the error message contains
  `"missing zone mapping"` and the op id.

Callers that previously relied on the agent-zone fallback must now
either (a) supply an explicit `zone` on the operation or (b) register
the tool in `ZoneRegistry` before calling `validate`.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
